Our campaign will be outraised.

**So let 's close out the month strong, close the gap between us and the other campaigns, and make sure we hit our April fundraising goal before the deadline -- chip in here:**

name: BernieSanders.com
subject: What. A. Day!
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Bernie 2020
body:
You’re going to want to see this.
# [](https://act.berniesanders.com/go/8915?t=1&akid=1194%2E150750%2EWPRzUG)

**If we can bring more people into our campaign, we are going to win. Visit[app.BernieSanders.com](https://act.berniesanders.com/go/11244?source=em190427-app&t=2&akid=1194%2E150750%2EWPRzUG) and use our new online tool — Bern — to look up people you know and let us know if they support our campaign or are undecided. This is so important.**
[USE THE TOOL](https://act.berniesanders.com/go/11244?source=em190427-app&t=3&akid=1194%2E150750%2EWPRzUG)

Sisters, Brothers, and Friends -
What an amazing day for our political revolution. More than 60,000 people attended more than 4,700 Organizing Kickoff events all across the country. And the message we sent loud and clear for all to see and hear is that this is **_the campaign**_ capable of defeating Donald Trump and transforming our country.
It was a shining example of the spirit animating our campaign: **_Not Me. Us.**_
A packed house in Seattle:
Good crowd in Bucks County, Pennsylvania -- a state we are going to win:
I stopped by an event in Southeast, D.C.
Not me. Cake.
A good group in Charlotte, North Carolina:
Impressive turnout of young people in Austin, Texas:
A lot of app activity in Nashville:
Michael Moore stopped by an event:
Ready to win in Louisville, Kentucky
We were thrilled to see events in more than two dozen countries around the world. There's a reason we did so well with Americans Abroad delegates last campaign. Here's an event in Rabat, Morocco:
We even heard about an event in Toronto that was held by a school band from the U.S. who were in Canada for an event but didn’t want to miss the big day.
At each of these events, people talked about the importance of growing our movement and building the largest grassroots organization this country has ever seen.
**And a big part of that effort is a new tool we introduced today. We call it BERN. It is a tool that lets you look up people you know and indicate if they support our campaign, are undecided, or support someone else.**
It’s an incredibly important ask. And it’s one we want to make of you today. Because organizing around personal relationships is a big part of how we are going to win. So I am asking:
**[Please visit this link, look up some of your friends and family members, and let us know if they support our campaign or are undecided. This is a critical ask. Please take a moment to do it today.**](https://act.berniesanders.com/go/11244?source=em190427-app&t=4&akid=1194%2E150750%2EWPRzUG)
People talking to people. It is our most valuable resource. And it is the foundation for how we are going to win this election and transform our country.
In solidarity,
Faiz Shakir
Campaign Manager
[USE THE TOOL](https://act.berniesanders.com/go/11244?source=em190427-app&t=5&akid=1194%2E150750%2EWPRzUG)

Paid for by Bernie 2020
PO BOX 391, Burlington, VT 05402
This email was sent to **dpwillis67@gmail.com**. If you need to, [**unsubscribe here**](https://bernie.actionkit.com/unsubscribe/unsubscribe?t=6&akid=1194%2E150750%2EWPRzUG). You can also [sign up to receive fewer emails](https://act.berniesanders.com/signup/fewer-emails/?t=7&akid=1194%2E150750%2EWPRzUG). We hope you'll stay, because emails are such an important part of how we organize and how we raise money. In fact, there is no single greater source for our campaign's fundraising than emails like this one. And that's important, because no one person, not even Bernie Sanders, can take on Trump and the billionaire class alone.

Before you go about the rest of your day, help power our fight against the billionaire class by [making a contribution today](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/bernie-email-template?refcode=em-footer&t=8&refcode2=1194_150750_WPRzUG&akid=1194%2E150750%2EWPRzUG).
Submit this form to [update your contact information](https://act.berniesanders.com/signup/update-info/?t=9&akid=1194%2E150750%2EWPRzUG).

Original Message from Jenn Liu, April 27, 2019

I couldn’t sleep last night.

It always happens this close to a fundraising deadline. Not only are we

We know we have the momentum -- big crowds, huge ratings during town
halls, and more.

But our April fundraising is still lagging behind and I’m running out of
options. Kamala is counting on you to get us back on track before this
critical deadline. So I have to ask:

Will you rush an urgent contribution -- whatever amount you can -- to help

United States?

subject: I'm emailing to ask you to make a donation before our April fundraising deadline. This one matters.

body:
| | |
---
| Friend, Our April fundraising deadline is in two days, and my team tells me we have some ground to make up to meet our budget goals. This deadline matters for campaigns like ours, who don't have the luxury of self-funding or cashing large checks from corporate PACs. Right now, we're seeing the first signs that our campaign has momentum -- we're even tied for first place in a _FiveThirtyEight_ study of Democratic activists in early states. But we also just saw the 19th and 20th candidates enter the race -- and the first debates are scheduled for a little over a month from now. **This is a moment we need to invest heavily in building this campaign. Every donation gets us one step closer to guaranteeing my spot on future debate stages.** [_**Make a donation of $3 or more today before our April deadline.**_](https://act.corybooker. name: Elizabeth Warren
subject: I'll just be blunt: Your donation would make a real difference right now
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Warren for President
body:
If we keep letting big money control our campaigns, we'll keep seeing Washington only work for people who can afford to hire armies of lobbyists and lawyers. | | | [](https://my.elizabethwarren.com/page/m/2852fe1d/45c30650/8e241477/6697a3f1/881930469/VEsH/ "https://elizabethwarren.com/")
---
I'll just be blunt: I'm about to ask if you can make a donation to our campaign. But first, can you give me a minute to explain why it would make a real difference to help hit our April fundraising goal?

Here's the way I see it: Our campaign is based on big ideas and a core principle -- everyone gets equal access because we're fighting for a government that works for everyone.

Our democracy is not for sale, and neither is my time.

So I'm opting out of the unwritten rule of politics that says I'm supposed to run for office by sucking up to rich donors on Wall Street and powerful interests in Washington.

I'm not hosting swanky fundraisers where you can only get in if you write a ginormous check. I'm not taking PAC money, donations from federal lobbyists, or applications from billionaires who want to run a Super PAC on my behalf.

**If we keep letting big money control our campaigns, we 'll keep seeing Washington only work for people who can afford to hire armies of lobbyists and lawyers.

We're fighting to change all that. We're building a grassroots movement to put power in the hands of the people, not the wealthy and well-connected.

[And it's only possible with contributions from people like you. I'm grateful from the bottom of my heart that you're on our team. Will you make a donation right now to help make sure we hit our April goal?](https://my.elizabethwarren.com/page/m/2852fe1d/45c30650/8e241477/6697a3f0/881930469/VEsE/p/eyJKU1ZsYldGcGJDVWwiOiJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSIsImUzc2dkWEpzSUgxOSI6ImV3LWFwcjE5ZW9tIiwiZTNzZ2NtVm1ZMjlrWlNCOWZRPT0iOiIyMDE5MDQyOGVtMS1mdWxsIn0=/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/ew-apr19eom?email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&refcode=20190428em1-full")**

When we hit our fundraising goals -- dollar by dollar, month after month -- and keep building our movement -- face to face, person to person -- we show the rich and the powerful that change is coming. Sooner than they think.

Billionaires, CEOs, big banks, and giant corporations won't always run the show in Washington.

I've got a plan -- a bunch of plans -- to level the playing field for working people and make sure we're building a future not just for some of our kids, but all of our kids.

If the rich and powerful don't like it, then tough.

We've got to make big, structural change -- we're _going to_ -- but it'll take a massive grassroots movement to get it done.

We're ready to dream big and fight hard. I see it in people's faces all across the country, including in South Carolina, Texas, Iowa, and Nevada in the past week.

**But first, our next big test is the fundraising deadline in a couple of days, and we need all hands on deck to hit our goal. If you can, please chip in:**

**[https://my.elizabethwarren.com/april-eom](https://my.elizabethwarren.com/page/m/2852fe1d/45c30650/8e241477/6697a3f3/881930469/VEsF/p/eyJKU1ZsYldGcGJDVWwiOiJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSIsImUzc2djbVZtWTI5a1pTQjlmUT09IjoiMjAxOTA0MjhlbTEtZnVsbCJ9/ "https://my.elizabethwarren.com/april-eom?email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&refcode=20190428em1-full")**

Thanks for being a part of this,

Elizabeth
| **[**DONATE NOW**](https://my.elizabethwarren.com/page/m/2852fe1d/45c30650/8e241477/6697a3f0/881930469/VEsC/p/eyJKU1ZsYldGcGJDVWwiOiJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSIsImUzc2dkWEpzSUgxOSI6ImV3LWFwcjE5ZW9tIiwiZTNzZ2NtVm1ZMjlrWlNCOWZRPT0iOiIyMDE5MDQyOGVtMS1mdWxsIn0=/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/ew-apr19eom?email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&refcode=20190428em1-full")**

| Team, I'm Danielle, the campaign's Digital Creative Strategist. I'm usually on the go, but today I'm taking a beat to write this email while my phone charges up--a pretty common thing since I'm constantly using it to take photos, videos, and notes that chronicle this campaign and the transformative conversations John is having every day on the trail.

**Watching him in action every day, I can tell you that John is a library of stories about the people he meets.** Whether you're a student worried about tuition bills, a senior worried about prescription drug costs, or a working parent reflecting on how you're going to get ahead, John will listen. He will empathize. He will remember what you said. He will talk it out with us in the car immediately after, and refer back to your stories long after we leave.

John is a storyteller. He is committed to finding every opportunity he can to tell your stories on the national stage--[**_and we 're especially focused on gaining the momentum and donor base we need to bring them to the debate stage in June._**](https://secure.hickenlooper.com/page/m/63771693/691c9bd/6c4e9de2/11a4a118/1146608811/VEsE/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/hickenlooper2020-em-apr?refcode=em_fr_20190428_DH_20190428-fr-DH-charged-ndnr_ndnr_l1&email=&firstname=&lastname=&city=&state=&zip=&phone=&occupation=&employer=")

Qualifying for the debates requires a combination of grassroots fundraising and donor count, and we need to grow both to make sure that John takes the stage at the first debate in Miami to offer a voice that our workers, our students, our seniors, and our immigrants need so deeply in this time of division.

[**_Can you step up to make your first donation now and keep John on track to advocate for everyday Americans at the debates?_**](https://secure.hickenlooper.com/page/m/63771693/691c9bd/6c4e9de2/11a4a119/1146608811/VEsF/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/hickenlooper2020-em-apr?refcode=em_fr_20190428_DH_20190428-fr-DH-charged-ndnr_ndnr_l2&email=&firstname=&lastname=&city=&state=&zip=&phone=&occupation=&employer=")

Charged up, ready to go,
Danielle

name: Abigail (personal)
subject: Doing what we said
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Spanberger For Congress
body:
As you already know, we've come a long way in a short time.
| | Peter,

I'm writing today to ask if you would be willing to make a contribution to my re-election campaign.

But before I ask for your help raising the last [**$16,781 we need to hit our April fundraising goal,**](https://act.myngp.com/el/Ru6iZdklYPeTaMtFA4P6wS9kfC65jp2gheuOMksk6T8=/314Bz1jpBOKKkNSdrnws87TIU_YliPdaLt4sJlZFgnE=?refcode=em_fr_2019.04.28_b1_as_all&amount=25&amounts=10,25,50,100,250,1000,2800) I'd like to fill you in on just how far we've come.

We don't have to look too far back to remember how it felt to have a representative who was afraid to meet constituents and refused to be accountable to voters.

And it wasn't that long ago that our district's representative was more committed to dismantling our healthcare system and blocking common sense gun violence prevention measures than he was to creating a more equitable economy.

A representative who was more passionate about rejecting the science of climate change than taking responsible steps toward protecting our shared environment.

As you already know, we've come a long way in a short time.

Since taking office, my team and I have worked to restore integrity to this role and redirect the privileges of this office away from rigid ideology and toward building a more cooperative, more compassionate approach to public service. I've been working hard to represent the people of our district, and that means, first and foremost, writing, championing and passing legislation that addresses the problems facing our country and our citizens. With my freshmen colleagues in Congress, we're proving people can, should and must expect more from their representatives.

But you also know that we didn't build a grassroots effort that won what the pundits called "unwinnable" overnight. It took months of building budgets, connecting with constituents, making calls, knocking doors, holding events and hitting **daily** , **weekly** , and **monthly** **fundraising goals** to cross the finish line victorious. In two days, we close the books on another one of these fundraising goals, but Peter, we still have to make progress towards our April 30th goal.
[**To be exact, we 're working to raise $16,781 by the end of the month -- which is why I'm coming to you now. Will you consider making a donation so we can reach our April target and pass another milestone together?**](https://act.myngp.com/el/Ru6iZdklYPeTaMtFA4P6wS9kfC65jp2gheuOMksk6T8=/54DlrhNy20PPMjHyeN48AtX2oqnzv3Q0hH5bvqPRrRw=?refcode=em_fr_2019.04.28_b1_as_all&amount=25&amounts=10,25,50,100,250,1000,2800) | [CONTRIBUTE »](https://act.myngp.com/el/Ru6iZdklYPeTaMtFA4P6wS9kfC65jp2gheuOMksk6T8=/54DlrhNy20PPMjHyeN48AtX2oqnzv3Q0hH5bvqPRrRw=?refcode=em_fr_2019.04.28_b1_as_all&amount=25&amounts=10,25,50,100,250,1000,2800)
---
I'm incredibly grateful to have a grassroots team that steps up when it counts. With your help, I'm confident this will be another one of those times.

Thank you for everything,
_\-- Abigail_
| Abigail Spanberger is a proud Democrat, former CIA officer, and representative for Virginia's 7th Congressional District. She believes in leadership that flows from our shared American values of integrity, responsibility, and decency, and she wants to work to move this country forward together. To learn more, please visit [AbigailSpanberger.com](https://act.myngp.com/el/Ru6iZdklYPeTaMtFA4P6wS9kfC65jp2gheuOMksk6T8=/d8nIX6DTlj2bGNl5dTlqqz7P0YSM81kGey3OyC_mLbg=)

If you'd only like to receive our most important email updates, you may [click here](https://act.myngp.com/el/Ru6iZdklYPeTaMtFA4P6wS9kfC65jp2gheuOMksk6T8=/iz6-jd-unBb0iDZdWCbPTN-Dc1Gb9DSlUZFD48HatQ0=) to request fewer emails from Abigail's campaign.

If you believe you received this message in error or wish to no longer receive email from us, please [unsubscribe](https://act.myngp.com/el/Ru6iZdklYPeTaMtFA4P6wS9kfC65jp2gheuOMksk6T8=/54LmUcw8DyGTfRHwHJNy3y-CVUzzwvJR6csKRDvv0Zo=).

It would be an honor to serve all Tennesseans in the U.S. Senate.
|

[ ](https://secure.jamesmackler.com/page/m/364aa29d/18d6a294/4aaf7e0e/26d698f2/4224966318/VEsH/ "https://www.jamesmackler.com/")

| | Team: It would be an honor to serve all Tennesseans in the U.S. Senate. And when I say that, the key word is **serve.** Unfortunately, too many politicians today are focused on serving themselves and the special interests that bankroll their campaigns. I believe in something different. That's why I closed my law practice and joined the U.S. Army after we were attacked on 9/11. I felt the need to do more in a time of crisis, and served my country. I feel the same way today and that's why I'm running to serve in the U.S. Senate. I know we're not going to fix healthcare, end the opioid epidemic, or create an economy that works in both rural and urban communities with our elected leaders tweeting insults and ignoring the needs of the very people they're elected to serve. That's not who I am or the kind of senator I'll be. But the truth is we're not going to get there without resources and right now we're behind our April goal. name: Robert Foster
subject: From the Trail
body:
Sunrise to Sunset
---
| | | | | | |
---
|
---
|
---
|
---
| Friend,
We've been working hard from before sunrise to well after sunset this week to earn Mississippians' support and trust to become their next governor (1,033 miles to be exact).
From Corinth to Houston to Hattiesburg to Oxford and back to our hometown this week sharing our vision for Mississippi and how I'll do my part to make our state a better place to live, hire an employee, and call home. I especially enjoyed being back in Oxford and my Alma mater for Double Decker weekend.
Campaigning across an entire state isn't easy. I knew that when we decided to jump in the race to be our state's next CEO, but I am reminded by it almost daily as I am away from my family, the family business and doing what I love on our farm.
Not having the big lobbyist and special interest money doesn't make it any easier, but it's the support and donations from average people that keeps us above water (sometimes barely) and moving forward!
The support you all have shown us and our message of the political outsider that is resonating with so many make the end result that much more worth it every week!
---
| From our family to yours, thank you for supporting us along the way. It means so much to us and we hope to see you along the trail.

Robert and Heather

clean_body: Sunrise to Sunset | | | | | | | | | | | Friend, We've been working hard from before sunrise to well after sunset this week to earn Mississippians' support and trust to become their next governor (1,033 miles to be exact). From Corinth to Houston to Hattiesburg to Oxford and back to our hometown this week sharing our vision for Mississippi and how I'll do my part to make our state a better place to live, hire an employee, and call home. I especially enjoyed being back in Oxford and my Alma mater for Double Decker weekend. Campaigning across an entire state isn't easy. I knew that when we decided to jump in the race to be our state's next CEO, but I am reminded by it almost daily as I am away from my family, the family business and doing what I love on our farm. Not having the big lobbyist and special interest money doesn't make it any easier, but it's the support and donations from average people that keeps us above water (sometimes barely) and moving forward! The support you all have shown us and our message of the political outsider that is resonating with so many make the end result that much more worth it every week! | From our family to yours, thank you for supporting us along the way. It means so much to us and we hope to see you along the trail. Robert and Heather | | | == | | | == == == | | | | == 12 West Commerce Street #295 Hernando, MS 38632 | | Friends of Robert Foster | 12 West Commerce Street #295, Hernando, MS 38632 | | Sent by mailto:robert@foster4ms.com in collaboration with |

name: Faiz Shakir, BernieSanders.com
subject: We’re a bit short
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Bernie 2020
body:
This is important.
# [](https://act.berniesanders.com/go/8915?t=1&akid=1196%2E150750%2Eewm0ZS)

Sisters and Brothers -
On Tuesday night at midnight, we close the books on an end-of-month fundraising deadline we’ve set for the campaign.
This is important, and I want to be transparent about why. The next morning, we are going to sit down and look at our fundraising numbers and make some important spending decisions about state investments, staffing levels, and advertising.
And right now, we are a bit short of where we hoped we’d be — especially in light of the big money flowing into our opponents’ campaigns this month.
So our job right now is to close the gap before this deadline ends. Every donation counts. And we’ll put each of them to work in a way that will help us win. So I am asking:
[**Can you make a $3 donation to our campaign before our end-of-month fundraising drive comes to an end? This is important.**](https://act.berniesanders.com/go/10584?refcode=em190428-1-15&t=26&akid=1196%2E150750%2Eewm0ZS)
So that’s the truth. We’re really hoping you can help us finish April strong. Because the strength of our campaign has always been the number of people who make it go. And if you choose today to make your first contribution since Bernie got in this race, it would mean a lot.
Thank you,
Faiz Shakir
Campaign Manager
